source_pr.title: dxf/importinto: Reset table mode on task done
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #66879, refer #61199

Problem Summary:
Flaky realtikv test `TestImportInto/TestImportSDK`: after `CANCEL IMPORT JOB`, table mode may still be `Import` until async cleanup, so the next `IMPORT INTO` can fail with `[schema:8258] Table t is in mode Import`.

### What changed and how does it work?
- Try to reset table mode back to `Normal` in import scheduler `OnDone` (classic kernel). Cleanup routine remains a fallback.
- Add a unit test to cover cancel path resetting table mode.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Table mode now resets to Normal earlier during import cleanup so tables are returned to normal use immediately after an import completes or is cancelled.

* **Tests**
  * Added tests to verify table mode is reset to Normal after import completion/cancellation to prevent regressions.

* **Chores**
  * Adjusted existing import test expectations to reflect the new timing of mode reset.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

symptoms: Flaky realtikv test `TestImportInto/TestImportSDK`: after `CANCEL IMPORT JOB`, table mode may still be `Import` until async cleanup, so the next `IMPORT INTO` can fail with `[schema:8258] Table t is in mode Import`.
failure_signature: Flaky realtikv test `TestImportInto/TestImportSDK`: after `CANCEL IMPORT JOB`, table mode may still be `Import` until async cleanup, so the next `IMPORT INTO` can fail with `[schema:8258] Table t is in mode Import`.
root_cause_explanation: The real-TiKV IMPORT INTO test was flaky because after `CANCEL IMPORT JOB` the table mode could remain `Import` until asynchronous cleanup ran, so the next import could fail with `Table ... is in mode Import`. The PR resets table mode to `Normal` on task completion (`OnDone`) so the state transition is deterministic for subsequent steps.
fix_pattern: Flaky realtikv test `TestImportInto/TestImportSDK`: after `CANCEL IMPORT JOB`, table mode may still be `Import` until async cleanup, so the next `IMPORT INTO` can fail with `[schema:8258] Table t is in mode Import`.
